flashcards.back: A positive whole number greater than 1 that has exactly two distinct factors: 1 and the number itself. For example: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, ...

flashcards.front: What is the only even prime number?
flashcards.back: 2. All other even numbers can be divided by 2, meaning they have more than two factors and are composite.

flashcards.front: Why is 1 not considered a prime number?
flashcards.back: Because a prime number must have exactly two distinct factors. The number 1 only has one factor (itself).

flashcards.front: What is the Sieve of Eratosthenes?
flashcards.back: An ancient algorithm for finding all prime numbers up to a certain limit by systematically crossing out multiples of primes starting from 2.

flashcards.front: Are there a finite or infinite number of primes?
flashcards.back: Infinite. Euclid proved that prime numbers continue forever without end.
